name: Suspicious Com Hijacking via Inprocserver32 Registry Modification
description: Detects potential COM hijacking activity where the InprocServer32 registry value under a CLSID key is modified to point to a DLL file located in temporary file directory. This technique is commonly used by malware, including Mythic agent variants, to achieve stealthy persistence and execution.

filterSets.name: Suspicious Com Hijacking via Inprocserver32 Registry Modification

author: Shaun Galura & Gerardo III Jonieca
